fields.helpText: Enter the locked data structure as set in the LOCK command, except:   1) If the lock is on a global data structure, enter a space before      the '^'.   2) If an array subscript is a variable, enter a legal variable name.     In examples 2-4, note that a space is necessary before the '^' of the global reference.     Example 1:    If the lock is on the local variable 'DPT', enter 'DPT'.     Example 2:    If the lock is on the entire ^DPT global, enter ' ^DPT'.       Example 3:    If the lock is o
This is the package for which the lock is set.  Locks usually are used to control access to a package's data.
Both local and global variables maybe locked.  This field explicitly identifies which type of variable the lock applies to. Global variables start with '^'.

If this field is set to YES, then a lock that has additional subscripts will be considered a match to this template as long as the first parts match.  A partial match will be allowed only if no full match exists.  For example, for the input template ^DPT(DFN), the lock on ^DPT(<dfn>,0) will be considered a match if this field is set to YES.
This multiple has an entry for each subscript in the locked data structure.  For example, a lock on ^DPT(<ien>,0) has two subscripts so should have two entries in this multiple.
This multiple provides information about the VistA files referenced by the locked data structure.
This field should describe how the lock is used and what its purpose is.
